properties.name: Tactical Pattern #1546
properties.wounding_strategy: Engine Rationale: Based on an analysis of 5947 conflicts spanning 3,000 years, the engine detected a 7-year cyclical tension threshold. Parallels historical mercantile blockades. Focuses on choking narrow maritime supply routes and subsea data cables. Target vector identified as unhardened Deep Sea Polymetallic Nodes.

properties.name: Tactical Pattern #4163
properties.wounding_strategy: Engine Rationale: Based on an analysis of 5947 conflicts spanning 3,000 years, the engine detected a 7-year cyclical tension threshold. Reflects ancient infrastructure denial tactics, updated for the digital age to target power grids and satellite downlinks. Target vector identified as unhardened Subglacial Resource Deposit.

properties.name: Tactical Pattern #6842
properties.wounding_strategy: Engine Rationale: Based on an analysis of 5947 conflicts spanning 3,000 years, the engine detected a 7-year cyclical tension threshold. Identifies protracted, multi-domain attrition zones designed to drain adversary resources without triggering conventional red lines. Target vector identified as unhardened Equatorial Aerospace Corridor.

properties.name: Western Sahara — Global Phosphate Choke
properties.wounding_strategy: 70%+ of world phosphate reserves concentrated here. Proxy conflict or blockade triggers global fertilizer price collapse → famine and economic inflation in rival nations within one harvest cycle. Slow-burn demographic weapon requiring zero kinetic engagement.

properties.name: Atacama Lithium Triangle — Battery Supply Severance
properties.wounding_strategy: Fund eco-sabotage, labor union weaponization, or port terminal acquisition across Chile/Bolivia/Argentina lithium corridor. Starves West and Asia of EV transition and next-gen military battery materials. Zero geopolitical fallout — appears as domestic labor disputes.
properties.strategic_impact: SEVERE — global EV and defense tech supply chain

properties.name: French Guiana / Kourou — Equatorial Aerospace Denial
properties.wounding_strategy: Diplomatic and economic coercion of equatorial South American nations denies rivals optimal satellite launch trajectories (Earth rotation advantage). No military action needed — commercial licensing, economic dependence, and sovereignty pressure force access denial to SpaceX and defense contractors.
properties.strategic_impact: HIGH — orbital superiority and satellite constellation dominance

properties.name: SEA-ME-WE Mediterranean-Arabian Fiber Node — Digital Financial Paralysis
properties.wounding_strategy: Grey-zone disruption of SEA-ME-WE cable bundle in Mediterranean or Arabian Sea segment. 'Rogue' fishing trawler anchor drag severs the fiber. India UPI, digital sovereign currencies, and real-time cross-continental banking freeze for hours-to-days. Zero casualties, maximum economic paralysis.
properties.strategic_impact: CRITICAL — freezes international digital payments and stock markets

properties.name: DRC Cobalt Transport Corridors — Battery Raw Material Choke
properties.wounding_strategy: Targeted proxy destabilization of the narrow road and rail corridors out of DRC cobalt belt. Buying up specific port terminals (Beira, Dar es Salaam) where cobalt leaves Africa. Starves global battery manufacturing. Appears as civil unrest — no direct military attribution.
properties.strategic_impact: SEVERE — global EV battery and military electronics supply

properties.name: Strait of Hormuz — Iran Digital Siege Zone
properties.wounding_strategy: IRAN CABLE WARFARE: ETH Zürich conflict models (Cederman & Pengl 2019) predict authoritarian states under sanctions develop asymmetric escalation capabilities below conventional war threshold. Iran has openly threatened cable severance — IRGCN operates in this 34km passage. A single operation disconnects UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain, Oman simultaneously. Rule R2 (Authoritarian + Economic Pressure → Asymmetric Escalation) triggers at 92% confidence. First use doctrine validated by Houthi proxy cuts Feb 2024.
properties.strategic_impact: CRITICAL — simultaneous digital and oil supply denial

properties.name: Sahel Ungoverned Space — IS/AQ Wave 3 Expansion
properties.wounding_strategy: Academic conflict wave model (Cederman & Pengl 2019) identifies IS caliphate collapse 2019 as triggering franchise diffusion into ungoverned Sahel space. Rule R2 (Authoritarian + Youth Unemployment → Islamist Recruitment) active across Mali, Niger, Burkina Faso. Non-state conflict deaths in MENA/Africa reached 14,000 in 2017 — trending upward. JNIM, IS-Sahel, Boko Haram filling governance vacuums. Predicted conflict arc: south through Nigeria coast and Central Africa within 5 years.
properties.strategic_impact: HIGH — regional state fragmentation; refugee flows to Mediterranean

properties.name: India-Pakistan Nuclear Flashpoint — Kashmir Trigger
properties.wounding_strategy: Cederman & Pengl 2019 identify India-Pakistan as highest nuclear instability risk globally: geographic proximity undermines crisis stability (unlike US-Russia separation). EPR data shows Kashmir population = discriminated ethnic group → Rule R1 (Ethnic Exclusion → Conflict Onset) at elevated probability. Military conventional exchange easily miscalculated as first-strike preparation. Nuclear Rule R5 triggers: IF conventional military loss imminent THEN first-use consideration = HIGH. Pakistan army doctrine explicitly includes tactical nuclear use against Indian conventional advance.
properties.timeframe: Perpetual — escalation window opens during crises
properties.strategic_impact: EXISTENTIAL — South Asian nuclear exchange risk

properties.name: MENA Negative Equilibrium Trap — Post-Arab Spring Arc
properties.wounding_strategy: Cederman & Pengl 2019 identify MENA as a structural 'negative equilibrium' — unique globally for its combination of high inequality, low ethnic inclusion (<70%), external power meddling (Russia, USA, Saudi, Israel), and authoritarian governance blocking reform. Rule R3 (Post-Conflict Poverty Trap) active: Syrian GDP 70% below counterfactual by 2016, cumulative losses $226B. Lebanon, Libya, Iraq, Yemen all exhibit the same structural pattern. Without inclusive governance reform + external pressure, the region cannot escape conflict recurrence. Academic prediction: continued fragmentation 2026-2035.
properties.strategic_impact: CRITICAL — continued mass displacement, regional state failure

properties.name: US Power Grid — Cascading Infrastructure Attack
properties.wounding_strategy: CISA assessed 2024: adversarial pre-positioning inside US grid control systems confirmed (Volt Typhoon / Salt Typhoon campaigns). China/Russia maintain persistent access to US power, water, and communications infrastructure for potential activation. A coordinated multi-node attack on the Eastern Interconnect (covering 70% of US population) during a geopolitical crisis could cause blackouts lasting weeks — no missiles required. The 2003 Northeast Blackout (accident) cascaded to 55M people in 2 days. A deliberate multi-point attack would be orders of magnitude worse.
properties.strategic_impact: CRITICAL — civilian paralysis, supply chain collapse, political destabilization

properties.name: US Electoral Infrastructure — Democratic Erosion Black Swan
properties.wounding_strategy: The most asymmetric attack on US power requires zero military engagement: undermine electoral legitimacy. Foreign interference (Russia 2016, China/Iran 2020) combined with domestic norm breakdown (Jan 6 2021) has already reduced institutional trust below 20%. The Black Swan: a 2026-2028 election where multiple states refuse federal certification, triggering a Constitutional crisis with no clear resolution mechanism. This would freeze US foreign policy, NATO commitments, and military command authority during the most dangerous geopolitical window since WWII.
properties.strategic_impact: CATASTROPHIC — US alliance system paralysis, adversary window of opportunity
properties.source_rule: Institutional Erosion Pattern + Foreign Interference Amplification

properties.name: SWIFT / US Dollar Reserve Bypass — Economic Sovereignty Attack
properties.risk: Academic Brain Prediction — STRATEGIC SHIFT UNDERWAY
properties.wounding_strategy: The US weaponization of SWIFT/dollar system (Russia sanctions 2022, Iran 2018, Venezuela) has created a 50+ country coalition actively building dollar bypass infrastructure. China's CIPS, Russia's SPFS, India's UPI cross-border, and the BRICS currency basket represent parallel financial architecture. The tipping point: when 30%+ of global trade settles outside dollar-denominated systems, US sanction power collapses and US Treasury borrowing costs spike. This is slow but structural — the most consequential long-term wound to US power projection capability.
properties.strategic_impact: SEVERE — US dollar reserve status erosion, sanctions power collapse

properties.name: Mexico-US Border — Cartel FTO Designation Escalation
properties.behavioral_strategy: Proxy State Capture + US Unilateral Action
properties.wounding_strategy: US FTO designation of Sinaloa/CJNG cartels (2025) creates legal basis for unilateral US military operations in Mexico. Cartels now control 50%+ of Mexican territory by governance capacity. A US Special Forces direct action mission inside Mexico — even against a cartel target — would trigger a Mexican sovereignty crisis, potentially collapsing USMCA, creating a hostile southern border, and providing China/Russia with a Western Hemisphere proxy conflict to exploit while US attention is divided. The asymmetric attack: provoke a US-Mexico rupture without firing a single shot at the US.
properties.threat_actor: Sinaloa Cartel / CJNG + China (fentanyl precursor supply chain leverage)
properties.strategic_impact: HIGH — USMCA collapse, southern border security crisis, US attention diversion
properties.source_rule: Proxy State Capture + Sovereignty Friction Pattern

properties.name: Iran Nuclear Threshold — Pre-emptive Strike Window
properties.wounding_strategy: IAEA confirmed: Iran at 60%+ enrichment, weeks from 90% weapons-grade. Cederman-Pengl Rule R5 (IF conventional military loss imminent THEN nuclear first-use consideration = HIGH) applies in mirror image to Israel: Israeli Air Force calculates a closing pre-emptive window. The 2024 direct Iran-Israel ballistic missile exchanges (April, October) have already crossed the direct-state-conflict threshold. IRGCN Strait of Hormuz interdiction + Houthi proxy cable cuts = Iran has validated every component of its asymmetric escalation doctrine. Missing only: nuclear deterrent finalization.
properties.threat_actor: Iran IRGC / IRGCN vs Israel IDF + US CENTCOM
properties.strategic_impact: EXISTENTIAL — regional nuclear war risk, global oil supply collapse

properties.name: Saudi Aramco — Infrastructure Paralysis via Houthi Proxy
properties.risk: Black Swan Wounding Zone — PROVEN CAPABILITY
properties.wounding_strategy: 2019 Abqaiq drone attack PROOF OF CONCEPT: took 5% of global oil supply offline in a single strike. Houthis have since acquired longer-range Iranian drones and ballistic missiles with improved precision. A sustained 30-day campaign targeting Ras Tanura (world's largest offshore oil loading facility, 7% of global daily oil supply) + Abqaiq reprocessing plant = catastrophic global energy shock. Iran achieves maximum strategic disruption through plausibly deniable Houthi proxy — zero direct attribution.
properties.strategic_impact: CRITICAL — 5-10% global oil supply disruption, energy price cascade
properties.source_rule: Proxy Infrastructure Denial + Proven Capability Escalation

properties.name: Malacca Strait — Taiwan War Chokepoint Activation
properties.wounding_strategy: If US-China conflict over Taiwan escalates to naval confrontation, the Malacca Strait becomes the primary US pressure point on China: 80% of Chinese oil imports transit here. A US Navy 7th Fleet Malacca interdiction order (blockade of Chinese commercial shipping) would be the economic equivalent of nuclear-level escalation — China's economy would contract 15%+ within 90 days. China knows this. Hence the Myanmar pipeline, Kra Canal pressure, and South China Sea island militarization — all are Malacca bypass strategies. Singapore's port (PSA International) is the digital control layer — a cyber attack here achieves blockade-equivalent disruption without a single warship.
properties.threat_actor: US Navy (interdiction) vs China (bypass / cyber counter)
properties.strategic_impact: CATASTROPHIC — 40% of global trade disrupted, China economic collapse trigger
properties.source_rule: Thucydides Trap Activation + Chokepoint Leverage Dynamics

properties.name: Myanmar Civil War — ASEAN Governance Vacuum
properties.wounding_strategy: Myanmar's military junta (SAC) has lost effective control of 70%+ of territory to resistance forces (PDF, ethnic armed organizations) as of 2025. This is not a frozen conflict — it is active state disintegration. Three consequences cascade regionally: (1) 2M+ refugees destabilizing Thailand, Bangladesh, India's Manipur; (2) China's $15B BRI investment in Myanmar (Kyaukphyu port, pipelines) now at risk — triggering potential Chinese military intervention to protect assets; (3) Drug production (world's largest opium producer since 2023) funding all armed factions, creating narco-state dynamics. Cederman-Pengl R3 (Post-Conflict Poverty Trap) fully activated: GDP 20% below 2019 levels, no recovery pathway.
properties.threat_actor: SAC Junta vs. PDF/EAO Resistance + China BRI protection interest
properties.timeframe: Active — 0–36 months to potential state collapse
properties.strategic_impact: HIGH — ASEAN security vacuum, refugee crisis, China intervention risk

properties.name: Thailand — Kra Canal Geopolitical Proxy Competition
properties.wounding_strategy: The proposed Kra Canal (120km across Kra Isthmus, southern Thailand) would create a new Malacca bypass — eliminating the need for ships to transit Singapore. China is the primary backer ($28B estimated cost). If built: Singapore loses 40% of port revenue and its strategic leverage; US loses Malacca as a China pressure point; Thailand becomes China's strategic partner. The decision is Thailand's — making it the most consequential infrastructure vote of the decade. A Chinese-backed Thai military government (post-coup) approving Kra Canal construction would represent a strategic rupture of the US-led Indo-Pacific order without a single shot fired.
properties.strategic_impact: SEVERE — Indo-Pacific trade architecture realignment, Singapore strategic displacement
properties.source_rule: Infrastructure Geopolitics + Thucydides Trap Regional Proxy
